title: Nursery Rhyme. CLXVIII. Songs.
content:
Where are you going, my pretty maid?
        I'm going a-milking, sir, she said.
        May I go with you, my pretty maid?
        You're kindly welcome, sir, she said.
        What is your father, my pretty maid?
        My father's a farmer, sir, she said.

        Say, will you marry me, my pretty maid?
        Yes, if you please, kind sir, she said.
        Will you be constant, my pretty maid?
        That I can't promise you, sir, she said.
        Then I won't marry you, my pretty maid!
        Nobody asked you, sir! she said.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CLXVIII. Songs."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
